title: Market Brief — Wednesday, April 22, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across every GPU class. B200 availability sits at a severe 7% (152/442 GPUs), H100 at a tight 15% (105/341), and H200 at 21% (43/310) — none above the 25% scarcity threshold. B200 spot crashed 18.4% overnight to $3.88/hr even as utilization surged to 92.5%, signaling a capacity buildout repricing against extreme demand; H200 spiked 10.2% on thin supply with 57.5% annualized volatility.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across every GPU class. B200 availability sits at a severe 7% (152/442 GPUs), H100 at a tight 15% (105/341), and H200 at 21% (43/310) — none above the 25% scarcity threshold. B200 spot crashed 18.4% overnight to $3.88/hr even as utilization surged to 92.5%, signaling a capacity buildout repricing against extreme demand; H200 spiked 10.2% on thin supply with 57.5% annualized volatility.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), H100 inference runs $7.08/M tokens and H200 hits $9.19/M tokens, making medium-tier API pricing a bloodbath. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.378/M output is selling at a loss of roughly $2.45/M tokens on H100 — grade: Burning Cash. Meta Llama 4 Maverick at $0.60/M is similarly underwater. OpenAI's o3 Pro at $80/M output prints 96% margins: Strong. Anthropic Haiku at $5/M is lean but viable: Neutral.

B200 spot at $3.88/hr is undervalued given 92.5% utilization and severe 7% availability — that overnight drop is a buying window before demand reprices it. H200 at $3.84/hr with 57.5% annualized vol and only 21% availability is overvalued relative to B200's superior cost-per-token economics — rotate H200 exposure into B200 contracts now.
bullets: 7% of B200 GPUs are available, the tightest supply across all three compute classes.
15% H100 availability with 85.2% utilization marks continued scarcity in the workhorse tier.
$0.378/M output pricing means DeepSeek V3.2 loses approximately $2.45 per million tokens after TCO adjustment.
92.5% B200 utilization surged 54.4 percentage points in 30 days, the fastest ramp in the dataset.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means API pricing has fallen 78.6% since GPT-4 launch baseline.
$80/M output on o3 Pro delivers 96% gross margin, the highest in the current 18-model universe.
